steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, $6t^{2}(31t-5u)$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: We need to factor $186t^3 - 30t^2u$ by pulling out the greatest common factor (GCF) from both terms.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $6t^{2}(31t^{2}-5u)$: the first term would become $186t^4$, not $186t^3$. ✗
  • Choice B — $6t^{2}(31t-5u)$: GCF of $186t^3$ and $30t^2u$ is $6t^2$. Factoring gives $6t^2(31t - 5u)$. Check: $6t^2 \cdot 31t = 186t^3$ and $6t^2 \cdot 5u = 30t^2u$. ✓
  • Choice C — $6tu(31t^{2}-5)$: multiplying gives $186t^3u - 30tu$, which does not match the original expression. ✗
  • Choice D — $6t^{2}u(31t-5)$: multiplying gives $186t^3u - 30t^2u$, with an extra $u$ in the first term. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
